message:
perf(search): reorder needle filter after label and timestamp predicates

The search query in `SqliteStorage::search_issues_with_filters` was
appending the lowercased needle clause

    AND (instr(lower(title), ?) > 0
         OR instr(lower(description), ?) > 0
         OR instr(lower(id), ?) > 0)

before it added the label `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM labels ...)` and
timestamp range predicates. SQLite generally evaluates AND-linked
predicates left-to-right and pushes them through the index/scan
pipeline in source order. Putting the per-row case-insensitive scan
first meant every candidate row paid for three `instr(lower(...))`
calls before the much cheaper indexed label/timestamp filters had a
chance to discard it.

Reorder so the needle clause is appended last, after the labels loop
and the four timestamp guards. The query plan now narrows the row set
via the indexed filters first and only invokes `instr` on the
already-trimmed candidate set. The needle parameter still uses the
existing `trimmed.to_ascii_lowercase()` allocation; semantics are
identical, only evaluation order changes.

No new public API and no schema change.
